publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 18:02:04 UTC.

ead.archdesc:
======= RMA01958Gould Colman papers13-6-1958Compiled by RMC StaffDivision of Rare and Manuscript Collections
September 20182B Carl A. Kroch LibraryCornell UniversityIthaca, NY 14853rareref@cornell.eduURL:
agricThis finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 18:02:32 UTC.12/07/2018
This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor) >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Gould Colman papers
Colman, Gould P. (Gould Patchin), 1926-
13-6-1958
7 cubic feet. 7 cubic feet.
1954-1996. Correspondence, Photographs, Printed Materials. Drafts, interview transcripts and other materials relating to a book project on family farming. Correspondence, materials relating to Rural Sociology courses he taught on Farm Families. Other materials relating to his time as the Cornell University Archivist. Collection material in English <<<<<<< HEAD 7 ======= 7 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab 2072203
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
RMC Staff
Date completed:
September 2018
EAD encoding:
Ben Wrubel, September 2018
Date modified:
Marcie Farwell, November 2018
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Archivist, historian.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Drafts, interview transcripts and other materials relating to a book project on family farming. Correspondence, materials relating to Rural Sociology courses he taught on Farm Families. Other materials relating to his time as the Cornell University Archivist.
NOTES
Also includes #13- -m.705, #21-2-m.401, #47-1-m.569, and #14-2-m.984.
SERIES LIST
Series I. Family Business
a. Interview transcripts
b. Drafts
c. Publication
d. Miscellaneous
Series II. Correspondence
Series III. Lectures, papers, and conference presentations
Series IV. Course materials
Series V. Miscellaneous
Cite As:
Gould P. Colman Papers, #13-6-1958. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Access Restrictions:
Access to farm family interview materials and Residential Club fire folder restricted to permission of the University Archivist.
Oral history -- Iowa. Oral history -- New York (State) Farmers -- Family relationships. Farm life -- Iowa -- History. Farm life -- New York (State) -- History. Family farms -- Iowa. Family farms -- New York (State) Farm management -- Iowa. Farm management -- New York (State) Farm management -- Decision making -- Case studies. Women in agriculture. Agriculture -- New York (State)
2072203 <<<<<<< HEAD Family Business1966-2011Interview transcripts1966-1982
Arrangement
Interview transcripts have been foldered, but left in original order.

Folder titles reflect names as listed in interview; names have been changed for "Family Business" book, and some names as listed on interviews are hybrids.Interview transcript excerpts - Reed family197711

Processing Information
Some by Gould Colman, others by colleagues. Authors marked where known.Rural Studies Group Fall Seminar Series syllabusUndated237American Farm Women Conference seminar participants list1984-02-04239"The Farmer Takes a Wife: Women in America's Farming Families" by Sarah Elbert1985240"Amber Waves of Gain: Women's Work in New York Families" by Sarah ElbertUndated241"Contented Cows and Dairy Maids: Patriarchy and Capitalism in American Agriculture" by Sarah Elbert1987255"Farming Families: The Farm Needs Everyone" by Gould Colman and Sarah ElbertUndated49"A Real Ranch" with commentsUndated612
Scope and Contents
No author listed, but possibly by Allen Bjergo and Tom Ruffatto."Ranching"1987-06614
Scope and Contents
No author listed, but possibly by Allen Bjergo and Tom Ruffatto.List of participants, Kellogg Workshop1987-11617"Contented Cows and Dairy Maids: Patriarchy and Capitalism in American Agriculture" by Sarah Elbert1987618"American Farm Women in Historical Perspective" conference materials1986-10623"Battle Plan for St. Paul"1987-10625Abstracts - Farm Women Program1982641Farm Stress group materials1985642"Summary of Major Findings from American Farm Women: Findings from a National Survey" by Calvin Jones and Rachel Rosenfeld1981643"The Historical Roots of Occupational Sexism" by Heidi Hartmann1976644"Inventory of the family's human resources and procedures for use"1980-01-16654"Art, Occupation, and Mystery: Making Decisions in U.S. Farming" article drafts and correspondence1980-198371"Art, Occupation, and Mystery: Making Decisions in U.S. Farming"198372"The Division of Labor in Families and the Development of Adolescent Farm Girls in the 1970s" by Janet Kalinowski1982-0573Papers for Rural Sociological Society conference, Farm Family session198074Farm Women press release1982-0875"Psychological Stress Among Farm Women" by Alan Berkowitz1982-1078"Farm Husbands and Wives in Wisconsin" by Eugene A. Scope and Contents
Photographs from the Alumni Affairs/Archives Photo Exhibit, used in Barton Hall during Reunion Week in 1987. They were available to Cornell class of 1987 alumni for purchase.Prints of historic Cornell photosUndated1
Scope and Contents
Photographs from the Alumni Affairs/Archives Photo Exhibit, used in Barton Hall during Reunion Week in 1987. They were available to Cornell class of 1987 alumni for purchase. ======= Family Business1966-2011Interview transcripts1966-1982

Scope and Contents
Photographs from the Alumni Affairs/Archives Photo Exhibit, used in Barton Hall during Reunion Week in 1987. They were available to Cornell class of 1987 alumni for purchase.Prints of historic Cornell photosUndated1
